title: Sicilian Defense: Dragon Variation
summary: Enter the Dragon, where Black builds a massive pawn storm on the kingside while White attacks on the queenside.
description: The Dragon is one of the most spectacular Sicilian variations. Named after the constellation, Black's pawn structure resembles a dragon. You'll learn the Yugoslav Attack (the main line) and understand why both sides push pawns aggressively on opposite wings.

variations.yugoslavAttack: 6.Be3 Bg7 7.f3 O-O 8.h4 - The main line with pawn storm
variations.classical: 6.Be2 Bg7 7.O-O O-O - A solid alternative without the early h4
variations.accelerated: 5...g6 instead of ...d6 first - The Accelerated Dragon

objectives: Understand the Dragon pawn structure and the strategic ideas on both sides
Learn the dangerous Yugoslav Attack (6.Be3 Bg7 7.f3)
Master the typical tactics: the h4-h5-h6 sacrifice and the rook lift
Recognize when to attack and when to defend in Dragon positions
plans: White's plan: Castle queenside, push h4-h5-h6, and attack with the rooks
Black's plan: Push f5-f4, ...h5, and attack on the kingside
The move ...d5 is Black's central break to free their game
White can also play on the queenside with b4-b5 after proper preparation
commonMistakes: Do not castle kingside against the Dragon - you'll be in for a rough ride
Avoid playing h4 too early without proper development
Do not underestimate Black's kingside attack - the Dragon is dangerous
The endgame typically favors White due to Black's weakened king position
completionTitle: Both sides have major attacking chances
completionSummary: The Dragon is a weapon of mass destruction. You've learned the main ideas and can now play both sides.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Play e4 to claim central space.
steps.coaching: The Dragon is one of Black's most aggressive Sicilian options.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black plays the Sicilian
steps.instruction: Black challenges the center with c5.
steps.coaching: The Dragon is an ultra-aggressive response.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. The knight controls e5 and g5.

steps.title: Black supports the center
steps.instruction: The pawn on d6 supports ...e5 and prepares development.
steps.coaching: Standard Sicilian development before entering Dragon structures.

steps.title: Open the center
steps.instruction: Play d4 to open the position.
steps.coaching: This is the main line, accepting the isolated pawn structure.
steps.hint: Push the d-pawn to challenge c5.

steps.title: Black exchanges
steps.instruction: Black captures on d4.
steps.coaching: Black gives up the d-pawn for development.

steps.title: Recapture with the knight
steps.instruction: Take back with the knight, developing.
steps.coaching: White has central control and better development.
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the knight.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6.
steps.coaching: Black completes development and attacks e4.

steps.title: Develop the other knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3 to complete development.
steps.coaching: White continues development. The knight controls d5 and e4.
steps.hint: Develop the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: The Dragon move
steps.instruction: Black plays g6, setting up the Dragon pawn structure.
steps.coaching: This is the Dragon! Black will develop the bishop to g7 in one move.

steps.title: The Yugoslav Attack begins
steps.instruction: Place the bishop on e3, preparing the attack.
steps.coaching: This is the Yugoslav Attack, White's most dangerous line against the Dragon.

steps.title: The Dragon Bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to g7, controlling the long diagonal.
steps.coaching: The Dragon bishop is very powerful, controlling e5 and c3.

steps.title: Support the center
steps.instruction: Play f3 to support e4 and prepare the attack.
steps.coaching: This move prepares the kingside pawn storm and secures the center.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.instruction: Black castles kingside.
steps.coaching: Both sides are now castled. The battle of the attacks begins.

steps.title: The pawn storm begins
steps.instruction: Push h4 to begin the kingside attack.
steps.coaching: This is the main line! White begins pushing h4-h5-h6.

steps.title: Black pushes back
steps.instruction: Black pushes h5, contesting the kingside.
steps.coaching: Black meets the attack with their own pawn storm.

steps.title: White advances
steps.instruction: Push h5 to open lines against Black's king.
steps.coaching: White continues the attack. The position is highly tactical.
steps.hint: Push h5 to open the h-file.

steps.title: Black captures
steps.instruction: Black captures on h5, opening the g-file.
steps.coaching: Black accepts the challenge. The position is now very complex.

steps.title: White captures back
steps.instruction: Take back with the rook, attacking.
steps.coaching: White has a strong initiative. The rook on h5 is very active.
steps.hint: Capture on h5 with the rook.

steps.title: Black's central break
steps.instruction: Black plays d5, opening the center.
steps.coaching: This is Black's standard break to free their game in the Dragon.
